hash2.name: hash2

fingerprint:compare.description: compare fingerprints of the current project, builds, and updates
examples: $ eas fingerprint:compare 	 # Compare fingerprints in interactive mode
$ eas fingerprint:compare <FINGERPRINT-HASH> 	 # Compare fingerprint against local directory
$ eas fingerprint:compare --build-id <BUILD-ID> 	 # Compare fingerprint from build against local directory
$ eas fingerprint:compare --build-id <BUILD-ID> --environment production 	 # Compare fingerprint from build against local directory with the "production" environment
$ eas fingerprint:compare --build-id <BUILD-ID-1> --build-id <BUILD-ID-2>	 # Compare fingerprint from a build against another build
$ eas fingerprint:compare --build-id <BUILD-ID> --update-id <UPDATE-ID>	 # Compare fingerprint from build against fingerprint from update
$ eas fingerprint:compare <FINGERPRINT-HASH> --update-id <UPDATE-ID> 	 # Compare fingerprint from update against provided fingerprint

build-id.description: Compare the fingerprint with the build with the specified ID
build-id.name: build-id

update-id.description: Compare the fingerprint with the update with the specified ID
update-id.name: update-id

open.description: Open the fingerprint comparison in the browser
open.name: open

environment.description: If generating a fingerprint from the local directory, use the specified environment.
environment.name: environment

json.name: json

non-interactive.description: Run the command in non-interactive mode.
non-interactive.name: non-interactive

fingerprint:generate.description: generate fingerprints from the current project
examples: $ eas fingerprint:generate  	 # Generate fingerprint in interactive mode
$ eas fingerprint:generate --build-profile preview  	 # Generate a fingerprint using the "preview" build profile
$ eas fingerprint:generate --environment preview  	 # Generate a fingerprint using the "preview" environment
$ eas fingerprint:generate --json --non-interactive --platform android  	 # Output fingerprint json to stdout

ref.description: The git reference must exist in the project's git repository, and the workflow file must exist at that reference. When this flag is used, the local project is not uploaded; instead, the workflow is run from the exact state of the project at the chosen reference.
ref.name: ref
ref.summary: Git reference to run the workflow on

json.description: Enable JSON output, non-JSON messages will be printed to stderr.
json.name: json

workflow:runs.description: list recent workflow runs for this project, with their IDs, statuses, and timestamps

workflow.description: If present, the query will only return runs for the specified workflow file name
workflow.name: workflow

status.description: If present, filter the returned runs to select those with the specified status
status.name: status

json.description: Enable JSON output, non-JSON messages will be printed to stderr.
json.name: json

limit.description: The number of items to fetch each query. Defaults to 10 and is capped at 100.
limit.name: limit

workflow:status.description: show the status of an existing workflow run. If no run ID is provided, you will be prompted to select from recent workflow runs for the current project.

non-interactive.description: Run the command in non-interactive mode.
non-interactive.name: non-interactive

wait.description: Exit codes: 0 = success, 11 = failure, 12 = canceled, 13 = wait aborted.
wait.name: wait
wait.summary: Wait for workflow run to complete. Defaults to false.

json.description: Enable JSON output, non-JSON messages will be printed to stderr.
json.name: json

path.description: Path to the workflow configuration YAML file (must end with .yml or .yaml)
path.name: path

workflow:validate.description: validate a workflow configuration yaml file

non-interactive.description: Run the command in non-interactive mode.
non-interactive.name: non-interactive

id.description: ID of the workflow run to view
id.name: id

workflow:view.description: view details for a workflow run, including jobs. If no run ID is provided, you will be prompted to select from recent workflow runs for the current project.

json.description: Enable JSON output, non-JSON messages will be printed to stderr.
json.name: json

non-interactive.description: Run the command in non-interactive mode.
non-interactive.name: non-interactive

update:configure.description: configure the project to support EAS Update

environment.description: Environment to use for the server-side defined EAS environment variables during command execution, e.g. "production", "preview", "development".
environment.name: environment

non-interactive.description: Run the command in non-interactive mode.
non-interactive.name: non-interactive

groupId.description: The ID of an update group to delete.
groupId.name: groupId

update:delete.description: delete all the updates in an update group

json.description: Enable JSON output, non-JSON messages will be printed to stderr. Implies --non-interactive.
json.name: json

non-interactive.description: Run the command in non-interactive mode.
non-interactive.name: non-interactive

groupId.description: The ID of an update group to edit.
groupId.name: groupId

update:edit.description: edit all the updates in an update group

rollout-percentage.description: Rollout percentage to set for a rollout update. The specified number must be an integer between 1 and 100.
rollout-percentage.name: rollout-percentage

branch.description: Branch for which to list updates to select from
branch.name: branch

json.description: Enable JSON output, non-JSON messages will be printed to stderr. Implies --non-interactive.
json.name: json

non-interactive.description: Run the command in non-interactive mode.
non-interactive.name: non-interactive

branch.description: Branch to publish the update group on
branch.name: branch

channel.description: Channel that the published update should affect
channel.name: channel

message.description: A short message describing the update
message.name: message

skip-bundler.description: Skip running Expo CLI to bundle the app before publishing
skip-bundler.name: skip-bundler

clear-cache.description: Clear the bundler cache before publishing
clear-cache.name: clear-cache

no-bytecode.description: Skip generating Hermes bytecode (output plain JavaScript instead)
no-bytecode.name: no-bytecode

source-maps.description: Emit source maps. Options: true (default), inline, false
source-maps.name: source-maps

emit-metadata.description: Emit "eas-update-metadata.json" in the bundle folder with detailed information about the generated updates
emit-metadata.name: emit-metadata

rollout-percentage.description: Percentage of users this update should be immediately available to. Users not in the rollout will be served the previous latest update on the branch, even if that update is itself being rolled out. The specified number must be an integer between 1 and 100. When not specified, this defaults to 100.
rollout-percentage.name: rollout-percentage

auto.description: Use the current git branch and commit message for the EAS branch and update message
auto.name: auto

private-key-path.description: File containing the PEM-encoded private key corresponding to the certificate in expo-updates' configuration. Defaults to a file named "private-key.pem" in the certificate's directory. Only relevant if you are using code signing: https://docs.expo.dev/eas-update/code-signing/
private-key-path.name: private-key-path

environment.description: Environment to use for the server-side defined EAS environment variables during command execution, e.g. "production", "preview", "development". Required for projects using Expo SDK 55 or greater.
environment.name: environment

json.description: Enable JSON output, non-JSON messages will be printed to stderr. Implies --non-interactive.
json.name: json

non-interactive.description: Run the command in non-interactive mode.
non-interactive.name: non-interactive

branch.description: List updates only on this branch
branch.name: branch

offset.description: Start queries from specified index. Use for paginating results. Defaults to 0.
offset.name: offset

limit.description: The number of items to fetch each query. Defaults to 25 and is capped at 50.
limit.name: limit

json.name: json

non-interactive.name: non-interactive

destination-channel.name: destination-channel

destination-branch.description: Branch name to republish to if republishing to a different branch
destination-branch.name: destination-branch

message.description: Short message describing the republished update group
message.name: message

Defaults to a file named "private-key.pem" in the certificate's directory. Only relevant if you are using code signing: https://docs.expo.dev/eas-update/code-signing/
private-key-path.name: private-key-path

rollout-percentage.description: Percentage of users this update should be immediately available to. Users not in the rollout will be served the previous latest update on the branch, even if that update is itself being rolled out. The specified number must be an integer between 1 and 100. When not specified, this defaults to 100.
rollout-percentage.name: rollout-percentage
